memory.summary: I have a free afternoon to complete a 3-hour scholarship application that could significantly reduce my term costs, or choose the immediate fun activity.
Starting the application early creates a buffer for potential friction, reducing Thursday stress. Since fun options remain available later, I can proceed with the task now.
I spent 2 hours on the scholarship application but only made minor progress. Half my free time is wasted, and I still have much work left before the deadline.
I wasted half my free time on a slow scholarship application, leaving the task incomplete and increasing my stress despite having ample energy.
I wasted 2 hours on a slow scholarship application, leaving only 0.65 progress despite a 3-hour deadline and increasing stress.
Despite low energy and high stress, I finally advanced the scholarship application from 0.65 to 0.71 progress in just 1.5 hours, moving closer to the 3-hour deadline.
I made significant progress on the scholarship application, moving from 0.71 to 0.77 in just 1.5 hours, but my energy is depleted and stress remains high.
After working 1.5 hours, progress reached 0.83 with only 1 hour remaining. Despite low energy and high stress, I am now on track to finish the application.
I reached 89% progress on the scholarship application in just 1.5 hours, staying ahead of schedule despite severe fatigue and high stress.
I reached 94% on the scholarship application in just 1.5 hours, staying ahead of schedule despite severe fatigue and high stress.
The scholarship application is now complete, confirming I finished ahead of schedule despite severe fatigue and high stress.
The scholarship application is fully complete, confirming I finished ahead of schedule despite severe fatigue and high stress.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
